title: Minor ii-V
subtitle: am7b5 to D7 into gm
promise: Hear and play the minor cadence that pulls Autumn Leaves back into G minor.
why: The minor ii-V is one of the most important sounds in jazz. Autumn Leaves gives you a clean, musical version of it without needing a theory dissertation in a trench coat.

voicings.note: Keep this tense and unresolved. It is a setup chord.
This chord wants to fall into G minor. Let the F# lead the ear.
Resolve simply. Do not decorate before the landing is clear.

practice: Play am7b5 -> D7 -> gm7 with no tempo.
Repeat one chord per bar at 60 BPM.
Listen for F# resolving into G minor.
End by playing the A-section shells from lesson 001.

listening.note: Listen for small voicings and clear harmonic gravity rather than speed.
